title: Euler's sum of powers conjecture is false for k=4

source_refs: Roger Frye, 1988 (counterexample search); Noam Elkies, 1987 (existence proof for k=4)

informal_statement: Euler's sum of powers conjecture states that for integers n > 1 and k > 1, if the sum of n positive integers each raised to the k-th power equals another integer raised to the k-th power, then k <= n. This packet disproves the k=4 instance via the explicit counterexample found by Roger Frye in 1988: 95800^4 + 217519^4 + 414560^4 = 422481^4, giving n=3 < 4.
informal_proof_idea: push_neg on the universally-quantified conjecture reduces to exhibiting n, b, a witnessing the sum-of-4th-powers equation with n < 4. Take n=3, b=422481, a = [95800, 217519, 414560]; norm_num discharges the positivity/cardinality side conditions and decide verifies the numeric equality 95800^4+217519^4+414560^4=422481^4 by kernel computation.
formal_statement_pp: theorem euler_sum_of_powers_false_for_k4 : ¬ (∀ (n b : ℕ) (_ : 1 < n) (a : Fin n → ℕ) (_ : ∀ i, a i > 0) (_ : ∑ i, (a i) ^ 4 = b ^ 4), 4 ≤ n)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet disproves ONLY the k=4 instance of Euler's sum of powers conjecture. The general conjecture remains open for k >= 6 (not addressed here or anywhere in this corpus); the upstream sibling-repo file's k>=6 statement (`eulers_sum_of_powers_conjecture`) is `sorry`/`research open`. Round-8 (`Wikipedia/`) triage found this file (`EulerSumOfPowers.lean`, tags=2, sorries=1) via the bulk grep-count shortcut established in round 7: BOTH `research solved`-tagged theorems in the file (`false_for_k4`, `false_for_k5`) are genuinely `sorry`-free, self-contained, verbatim-transportable proofs -- no custom types, no infrastructure, exactly the Erdos-lane win pattern. Transported essentially verbatim from the upstream proof term, kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt (episode 67410574-3463-4255-b72a-b4f1b430e410). `statement_fidelity: canonical_statement_hash_match` since `problem_create`'s returned `root_statement_hash` was not independently cross-checked against an upstream-cited hash (none was cited in the source comment), but the statement is a character-for-character transcription of the upstream Lean source with only whitespace normalization. Companion packet: `frontier.formal_conjectures.euler_sum_of_powers_false_for_k5.v1` (same file, k=5 case, authored together this cycle). `open_problem_related: true` since both are specific-k instances of a conjecture family that remains open for k>=6 in the literature and in this corpus, following the same convention as `degiorgi_conjecture_dim_one.v1`.
